channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week following an initial court hearing in New York on Wednesday over the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia track athlete facing assault charge after baton strike she said was accidental
item.description: Alaila Everett said it was an accident but court records show she is now facing a potential assault and battery charge.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: Indonesian fishermen sue Bumble Bee and say the canned tuna giant knew of abuse in its supply chain
item.description: Lawyers representing four Indonesian fishermen say they were beaten and trapped on vessels that were part of the global supply chain that provided tuna to Bumble Bee Seafoods.
item.dc:creator: Julie Watson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How to photograph the total lunar eclipse
item.description: You could see the full moon turn blood red (or burnt red) early Friday morning as the earth transits between the moon and the sun. And if luck is on your side, there will not be any clouds covering this celestial sight.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Phenix, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI warns of extortion and self-harm networks targeting minors
item.description: FBI Denver said that 764, a decentralized and transnational sextortion network, is seeing an increase in activity, as well as other violent online networks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina House Republicans fight with each other over the budget
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — At least three-quarters of South Carolina's House members approved the more than 100 sections of the state's $14 billion spending plan this week, but is wasn't as easy as that makes it seem.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Risks of state abortion reporting mandates outweigh the benefits, an advocacy group says
item.description: States should stop requiring health providers to file reports on every abortion because the information poses a risk to both them and their patients in the current political environment, a research group that advocates for abortion access says.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump is dismantling the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job.” Since Trump took office, staffing at the Education Department has been cut in half, and he has been mulling an executive order to close the agency.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: One student asked a search engine, “Why does my boyfriend hit me?” Another threatened suicide in an email to an unrequited love. A gay teen opened up in an online diary about struggles with homophobic parents, writing they just wanted to be themselves.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE BRYAN of The Seattle Times and SHARON LURYE of The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tiger Woods says he ruptured his Achilles tendon
item.description: It's yet another injury that would seem likely to keep him out of the Masters and perhaps other major championships this year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote was not the decisive one, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, ABC 4

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DHS ending collective bargaining for transportation security officers
item.description: "They gave as a justification a completely fabricated claim about union officials," AFGE National President Everett Kelley said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: TROY, Mich. (AP) — Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility, Michigan’s attorney general said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: “A single spark it appears ignited into a fully involved fire that claimed Thomas’s life within seconds,” Attorney General Dana Nessel said.
item.dc:creator: Daylyn Huff, NewsNation

item.title: Infowars employee killed in Texas, law enforcement confirms
item.description: When police arrived, they found a man with a gunshot wound in the complex’s parking lot, Austin Police Department Detective Leah Ratliff said.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, NewsNation

item.title: No evidence Dane Paulsen's disappearance is related to white van, authorities say
item.description: Evidence suggests that Paulsen’s disappearance is not related to any crimes, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County, Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, Fox 5

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles County said Monday that he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez because the brothers have repeatedly lied about why they killed their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk eyes Social Security and benefit programs for cuts while claiming widespread fraud
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk pushed debunked theories about Social Security on Monday while describing federal benefit programs as rife with fraud, suggesting they will be a primary target in his crusade to reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge halts effort to deport Columbia protest leader, sets Wednesday hearing
item.description: A federal judge on Monday scheduled a Wednesday court hearing for Mahmoud Khalil, a recent Columbia University graduate who was arrested by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), forbidding the government from moving forward with deportation efforts before then.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security to again recoup 100% of overpayments
item.description: The Office of the Chief Actuary estimated the withholding rate increase could save about $7 billion over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Immigration agents arrest Palestinian activist who helped lead Columbia University protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal immigration authorities arrested a Palestinian activist Saturday who played a prominent role in Columbia University’s protests against Israel, a significant escalation in the Trump administration’s pledge to detain and deport student activists.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pushes to shut down the Dept. of Education: What impact it has on Florida
item.description: Florida relies on billions in Federal education funding, from special education to student loan programs.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader Sunday as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump isn't alone in his geopolitical aspirations. Some in Illinois and Oregon want change too
item.description: As President Donald Trump floats grand ideas like reacquiring the Panama Canal and taking over Greenland, some rural residents of Illinois and Oregon are promoting geopolitical change of their own: They want to break away from their states, and perhaps unite with Indiana and Idaho.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4
